title: The Singer.
content:
While with Ambition's hectic flame
         He wastes the midnight oil,
        And dreams, high-throned on heights of fame,
            To rest him from his toil, - 

        Death's Angel, like a vast eclipse,
            Above him spreads her wings,
        And fans the embers of his lips
            To ashes as he sings.
meaning:
This evocative piece by James Whitcomb Riley, titled "The Singer.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
